title: Perspective
evidence_text:
Soft polymer networks have seen an explosion
of recent developments motivated by new high tech
applications in the biomedical field or in engineering. We
present a candid and critical overview of the current
understanding of the relation between the structure and
molecular architecture of polymer networks and their
mechanical properties, restricting ourselves to soft networks
made of flexible polymers and displaying entropic elasticity.
We specifically review and compare recent approaches to

recoverability, and self-healing ability. While conventional complementary
multidentate H-bonding groups are advantageous for enhancing binding strength
due to their rigid structures, flexible multidentate H-bonding groups such as
aliphatic diols have recently been found to achieve a good balance between
dynamicity and mechanical robustness. In this study, we
